title: Codex runtime fitting (full secondary) — CodexAdapter over `codex exec` (clean non-PTY, prompt via stdin) + runtime kind + primary→secondary coding delegation

tests.summary: 9 passed — buildExecArgs: `codex exec`, model via `-c model=`, cwd via `--cd`, prompt via STDIN ('-', never argv); CodexAdapter passes the RuntimeAdapter conformance harness (stub exec); the adapter feeds the prompt to codex exec via stdin (asserted prompt NEVER in argv); the Codex secondary validates the spec + returns schema-valid {summary, artifacts} + writes output to the Artifact Store + logs the delegation (runtime-bridge-ok); the primary integrates the Codex summary (secondary-delegate-ok); rejects a model outside the Codex allowlist (loud). Full suite 580 passed / 9 skipped / 0 failed.

validateFitting.summary: PASS — faculty sessions (cli-skill), provides runtime:codex; the new `runtime` capability kind added to src/lib/types.ts + CAPABILITIES.md + METADATA.md (docs-consistency green).

build.summary: Next build compiled — runtime kind enum change is in src/lib/types.ts, all routes render

e2e.summary: N/A — delegation asserted via env/args + stub exec (brief: no live third-party round-trip required); committed tests + --probe are the re-runnable gate.

by: N/A — no UI (Quarters-Codex base is MRr-quarters)

verdicts.mix-and-match-ok: contract-level (runtime kind is symmetric; explicit composition primary/secondary wiring deferred)

blockers: mix-and-match-ok (Codex primary + Claude-Code secondary) is supported at the CONTRACT level — the `runtime` capability kind + RuntimeAdapter + runtime-bridge are symmetric, so any runtime can be primary or secondary. The explicit composition `primary`/`secondary` role field + wiring the secondaries' bridges into the primary's pooled sessions is the runtime-pool integration step (MRr-pool-multi), deferred. Logged, not silently dropped.

Live codex exec round-trips are asserted via argv/env + stub exec (the brief explicitly permits this — 'no real provider calls required to pass'); a live codex delegation is available via the bridge CLI but not run in the gated suite.
notes: codex-runtime is a SECONDARY runtime fitting proving coding delegation. CodexAdapter implements the RuntimeAdapter contract over `codex exec` — a CLEAN non-PTY channel (the brief's 'a future runtime with a cleaner channel than PTY may use it'): prompt via stdin (shell-injection-safe under bypassPermissions), no TUI scraping. buildExecArgs is pure/asserted; runExec is injectable so tests are deterministic without a live codex. The bridge CLI (scripts/bridge.mjs) reads the task spec from stdin, runs the generic delegate() core with the CodexAdapter, writes to the Artifact Store (artifacts.py when present, else local), logs to decisions.jsonl, --probe. The `runtime` capability kind was added (data-source/automation-runner precedent) — codex-runtime provides runtime:codex under the sessions role. RETRY 1: YAML colon-in-unquoted-command parse error. RETRY 2: faculty sessions rejects shape `cli`; switched to `cli-skill`. Satisfies brief §3 tokens secondary-delegate-ok + runtime-bridge-ok (via the Codex secondary); mix-and-match-ok at contract level.
